user_prompt: if there are 0 devices, then just idle forever here
choke the FIFOs going to the file because we are not using AudioFileRecorder at all in this test
connect to the first device in the list as a HFP
and just pump audio from Bluetooth callback to the speakers forever
implement the rest of the code, like #includes other scaffolding that I missed
agent_reply: - Initializes Serial, NVS, BluetoothManager, and AudioManager
- Prints local BT address, PIN, BT state, bonded device count/list, and connection status
- Idles forever when no bonded devices exist
- Connects HFP to the first bonded device
- Pumps Bluetooth audio to speaker forever
I also added the missing declaration in [all_tests.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.h).
Note: [all_tests.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.cpp) still calls `test_btpairing()`. To run this new test, switch `run_test()` to call `test_btspeakerphone()`.
- Initializes Serial, NVS, BluetoothManager, and AudioManager
- Prints local BT address, PIN, BT state, bonded device count/list, and connection status
- Idles forever when no bonded devices exist
- Connects HFP to the first bonded device
- Pumps Bluetooth audio to speaker forever
I also added the missing declaration in [all_tests.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.h).
Note: [all_tests.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.cpp) still calls `test_btpairing()`. To run this new test, switch `run_test()` to call `test_btspeakerphone()`.

user_prompt: move literally all insstances of idle_forever into `lib\UtilFuncs\utilfuncs.cpp`, it doesn't need to be fancy
agent_reply: Done. `idle_forever()` now lives in [utilfuncs.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.cpp) with its declaration in [utilfuncs.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.h), and the local copies were removed from all the test files.
It’s intentionally plain now: just loops forever with `delay(1000)`.

user_prompt: C:/Users/frank/.platformio/packages/toolchain-xtensa-esp-elf/bin/../lib/gcc/xtensa-esp-elf/13.2.0/../../../../xtensa-esp-elf/bin/ld.exe: .pio/build/theflybringuptest/firmware.elf section `.iram0.text' will not fit in region `iram0_0_seg'
C:/Users/frank/.platformio/packages/toolchain-xtensa-esp-elf/bin/../lib/gcc/xtensa-esp-elf/13.2.0/../../../../xtensa-esp-elf/bin/ld.exe: IRAM0 segment data does not fit.
collect2.exe: error: ld returned 1 exit status
ouch, we haven't even implemented half of the project yet
agent_reply: The mSBC undefined symbol is gone by changing [bluedroid_sbc.c](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/bluedroid/bluedroid_sbc.c) to use `OI_CODEC_SBC_DecoderReset(...)` and then set `mSBCEnabled = TRUE`, instead of calling the missing `OI_CODEC_mSBC_DecoderReset`.

The IRAM overflow was the ESP32 classic BT plus PSRAM combo. I disabled `BOARD_HAS_PSRAM` only for `[env:theflybringuptest]` in [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini), which frees enough IRAM for this bringup test without changing the base env.
Also finished the previous cleanup: `print_bda` is gone from the BT tests; they use `log_bda`.

user_prompt: inside `test_btspeakerphone`, please start another thread, in the same manner as in `src\tests\test_micrec.cpp`
leave the thread as a blank placeholder, do not test build, thanks
agent_reply: Added the pinned core 0 placeholder thread in [test_btspeakerphone.cpp](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_btspeakerphone.cpp).
- placeholder task body that just logs and idles forever
Added the pinned core 0 placeholder thread in [test_btspeakerphone.cpp](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_btspeakerphone.cpp).
- placeholder task body that just logs and idles forever

user_prompt: initialize the LCD display and the buttons
as in the other test, button-A is the push-to-talk button, button-C ends the test (disconnects bluetooth and idle_forever)
this has to signal to the other thread to do these things
from within this thread, do the screen colour changes, have the other thread signal back what colour is wanted
Red is connected and mic active
if there are super safe FreeRTOS mechanisms to do these thread safe signalling, demonstrate them. Personally I would've just declared variables and written them.
agent_reply: - Core 0 task now initializes LCD/buttons, owns `M5.update()`, button reads, and screen colour changes.
- Button A sends push-to-talk commands via a FreeRTOS queue.
- Button C sends a stop command via the same queue.
- Main thread receives commands, switches speaker/mic mode, disconnects Bluetooth on stop, and sends desired colour back through a one-slot FreeRTOS colour queue.
- Grey = idle/done, blue = connected/speaker mode, red = mic active.
Verified with `pio run`: build succeeds. `src/version.c` nonce regenerated again.

commit_message: speaker phone test more complicated controls

user_prompt: in `src\tests\test_btspeakerphone.cpp`, show a list of bonded devices before `src\tests\test_btspeakerphone.cpp`, similar to code in `src\tests\test_btpairing.cpp`
agent_reply: Done. In [test_btspeakerphone.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_btspeakerphone.cpp), I added a `print_bonded_devices()` helper matching the pairing test style, and call it right after Bluetooth init, before selecting the first bonded device to connect.
I also removed the list-printing side effect from `get_first_bonded_device()` so the log stays clean.
Verified with `pio run -e theflybringuptest`: build succeeds.

user_prompt: convert `get_first_bonded_device` to `get_nth_bonded_device` with a parameter, use 0 for now
agent_reply: Done. [test_btspeakerphone.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_btspeakerphone.cpp) now has `get_nth_bonded_device(int index, esp_bd_addr_t device)`, and the speakerphone test calls it with `0` for current behavior.
Verified with `pio run -e theflybringuptest`: build succeeds.

user_prompt: I've made changes to `src\tests\test_btspeakerphone.cpp` related to `USE_SPECIFIC_BDADDR` and `USE_SPECIFIC_BONDED_IDX`, please review and fix mistakes
agent_reply: Fixed the macro selection path in [test_btspeakerphone.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_btspeakerphone.cpp).
- Corrected `USE_SPECIFIC_BDADDR` from dash format to colon format, since `parse_mac()` expects `AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF`.
- Added a compile-time guard so `USE_SPECIFIC_BDADDR` and `USE_SPECIFIC_BONDED_IDX` cannot both be enabled.
- Added `select_target_device()` to keep the target choice in one place.
- Always prints the bonded device list before choosing a target.
- Checks `parse_mac()` result now, instead of silently using an all-zero target if parsing fails.
Verified with `pio run -e theflybringuptest`: build succeeds.

that kind of worked, I didn't hear anything, but I think the connection workflow worked
please add some diagnostic counters on the audio callback, have the test code report the counters every 2 seconds, use ESP_LOG because I think it includes a timestamp
agent_reply: Done. I added `AudioManager::HfpAudioDiagnostics` plus snapshot/reset APIs in [AudioManager.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.h:33), and wired counters through HFP incoming/outgoing audio plus speaker I2S writes in [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:718).

The speakerphone test now resets those counters before the pump loop and logs them every 2 seconds with `ESP_LOGI` from [test_btspeakerphone.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_btspeakerphone.cpp:75). You’ll get three lines: incoming decode/FIFO stats, outgoing mic stats, and speaker I2S/FIFO state.

I am trying to use a recorder app on my phone, it has a switch that explicitly says to use the headset mic, which I've enabled. I am pushing the button to activate the mic. I don't see the audio going through, and I think those messages in the log say the callbacks never happen?
agent_reply: Yes, your read is right: the outgoing callback never fires in that log.

That means Bluedroid never called `AudioManager::hfp_outgoing_audio()`. The mic FIFO may be getting filled by `pump_mic2bt()`, but the Bluetooth stack was never being told “I have outgoing audio ready, come fetch it.”

ESP-IDF’s HFP outgoing path is demand-triggered: after app code prepares mic data, it must call `esp_hf_client_outgoing_data_ready()`. You already had a wrapper for that, `BtManager::notifyOutgoingAudioReady()`, but `test_btspeakerphone` was not calling it.
I patched [test_btspeakerphone.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_btspeakerphone.cpp:568) so mic mode now does:

On the next run, when you hold PTT and the SCO audio link is up, you want to see `audio diag out` change from `cb=0` to nonzero, and `req`/`ret` should start moving. If `cb` moves but `ret` stays zero, then we’re into mic capture/FIFO data problems; but right now the first bug was simpler: nobody rang the dinner bell.

agent_reply: Done. The notification now lives inside [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:787), immediately after `pump_mic2bt()` queues mic samples into `g_fifo_mic2bt`.
I also added `BtManager::canNotifyOutgoingAudioReady()` in [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp:993). The notification is only allowed when:
The test loop no longer owns that logic; it just calls `AudioManager::pump_mic2bt()` again.
Verified with `pio run -e theflybringuptest`: build succeeds.

user_prompt: that didn't fix it, still 0 callbacks, add diagnostic prints for `audio diag mic` so we also count I2S reads and queued samples, enough information to know if we are meeting the conditions for `notifyOutgoingAudioReady`
agent_reply: Added `audio diag mic` logging and the underlying counters.
- mic pump calls and skip reasons: not mic mode / no I2S / FIFO full
- I2S read calls, requested/read bytes, empty reads, read errors, raw samples
- notify condition checks, ready count, actual notify calls
- failure buckets for notify: no queued data / below minimum / Bluetooth not ready
- current mic FIFO readable samples, notify minimum, and current `can_bt`
On the next log, the key read is:
If `call` increments but `audio diag out cb` stays `0`, then we are notifying Bluedroid but it still is not fetching outgoing audio.

user_prompt: apply the fix for the full fifo case, but also, letting the mic2bt fifo fill up is a problem. Queuing mic2file should be allowed but queuing mic2bt should be choked unless we are certain that Bluetooth audio is ready to push into
agent_reply: - `mic2file` keeps getting queued whenever mic samples are read.
- `mic2bt` is cleared and not queued while `BtManager::canNotifyOutgoingAudioReady()` is false.
- If Bluetooth is ready but `mic2bt` is full, `pump_mic2bt()` now attempts `notifyOutgoingAudioReady()` instead of returning early.
- `mic2bt` only receives new mic samples when Bluetooth is ready and the FIFO has write space.
- `bt_drop notready/full=.../...` was added to `audio diag mic`.
- `notready` counts mic samples intentionally not queued to Bluetooth because HFP audio was not ready.
- `full` counts mic samples that could not fit into `mic2bt` while Bluetooth was ready.
In the next log, the important thing is whether `q_bt` starts increasing only after `can_bt=1`, and whether `notify ... call` increments.
- `mic2file` keeps getting queued whenever mic samples are read.
- `mic2bt` is cleared and not queued while `BtManager::canNotifyOutgoingAudioReady()` is false.
- If Bluetooth is ready but `mic2bt` is full, `pump_mic2bt()` now attempts `notifyOutgoingAudioReady()` instead of returning early.
- `mic2bt` only receives new mic samples when Bluetooth is ready and the FIFO has write space.
- `bt_drop notready/full=.../...` was added to `audio diag mic`.
- `notready` counts mic samples intentionally not queued to Bluetooth because HFP audio was not ready.
- `full` counts mic samples that could not fit into `mic2bt` while Bluetooth was ready.
In the next log, the important thing is whether `q_bt` starts increasing only after `can_bt=1`, and whether `notify ... call` increments.

there's a lot of `E (24465) i2s_common: i2s_channel_read(1257): The channel is not enabled` in the logs, probably because the mic is being pumped without being in mic mode? easy fix for you I think
agent_reply: - the 2-second `audio diag ...` logs in [test_btspeakerphone.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_btspeakerphone.cpp:54) are not compiled

I also addressed the `i2s_channel_read(...): The channel is not enabled` spam. `stop_i2s()` now marks the mode stopped before disabling channels and takes the same pump mutex, while `pump_mic2bt()` serializes the I2S read against that lifecycle path in [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:494) and [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:832). Bluetooth outgoing-ready notification is deferred until after that lock is released.

Verification: `git diff --check` passes. Full `pio run -e theflybringuptest` is currently blocked by an unrelated `managed_components/espressif__libsodium` compile error around `SODIUM_C99`, before this code gets a clean app-level build.

commit_message: cleaned up diagnostics a bit, added mutex for pump functions

user_prompt: fix mistakes in recent changes, wrap the init calls being highlighted to report errors with ESP_LOGE
agent_reply: I changed [src/main.cpp](</mnt/d/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp>) so `AudioManager::init()` and `BtManager::init()` report failures with `ESP_LOGE`. I did not keep a separate `AudioFileRecorder::init()` call because `AudioManager::init()` now initializes the recorder internally and reports failure if that part fails. `BattTracker::init()` returns `void`, so there’s nothing meaningful to wrap.
I also fixed the split init code:
- [src/all_init.cpp](</mnt/d/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp>) now initializes M5 instead of touching `gui` before it exists.
- Added the missing splash sprite include.
- Fixed `init_nvs()` declarations in the Bluetooth tests from `void` to `bool`.
Verification: `pio run -e theflybringuptest` now succeeds. There are only third-party warnings. Note: `src/all_init.cpp` is currently untracked, but the build now depends on it.

user_prompt: I need both a new enum item for when audio is available, and a callback event that is fired to the existing `StateChangedCallback`
agent_reply: and `StateChangedCallback` will receive it when HFP audio routing actually comes up, from either CVSD or mSBC audio state events.
I also made audio disconnect transition back to `Connected`, so the sequence should now look like:
Connecting -> Connected -> Audio Available -> Connected -> Idle/Reconnecting

I updated internal Bluetooth checks so `Connected` and `AudioAvailable` both count as an HFP control connection where appropriate, while `canNotifyOutgoingAudioReady()` still requires real audio availability. The speakerphone test now treats both states as connected for controls.

commit_message: change bluetooth init behavior to be connectable

user_prompt: avoid having `M5GFX& display` as a parameter for functions and methods, there should be a global reference to the display for anything unrelated to FlyGui, and if FlyGui is involved, there should be a way to dig through trees to get a reference to it, or just use the global reference
agent_reply: - Updated direct display users to use `thefly_display` instead of threading a local `display` reference or calling `M5.Display` directly.
- Kept `FlyGui::display()` as a convenience getter that returns the global display, so tree-based GUI code can still ask the GUI for its screen if needed.

user_prompt: thanks, it works now, and that's why I wanted the test
the SBC stuff (the library directories and the entries in the platformio.ini file) have been removed from the project, but there's related code remaining, remove it
agent_reply: Cleaned out the leftover custom SBC codec plumbing.
- Removed stale SBC include paths from [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini).
- Removed SBC encode/decode diagnostics fields from [AudioManager.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.h).
I intentionally left the `HfpCodec::Msbc` path/name intact because ESP-IDF/Bluedroid can still report an mSBC HFP connection; the app now simply assumes the callback payload is PCM, as intended.
- `rg` finds no remaining `lib/sbc`, `lib/bluedroid`, custom SBC library macros/includes, or encode/decode diagnostic leftovers.
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` compiled sources but failed at link with the familiar `.iram0.text` overflow by `2104 bytes`.

user_prompt: bulletproof `get_random_wifi_channel` for me then make all the soft AP initialization use it
agent_reply: `get_random_wifi_channel()` now only selects from `{1, 6, 11}`, has a fallback, mixes `esp_random()`, `millis()`, and `micros()`, and can’t return an invalid AP channel. The WPA3 SoftAP path now chooses a channel once via that helper and passes it into `configure_soft_ap_security()` instead of hardcoding channel 1.
I also added a log line showing the chosen AP channel.
Build passes. The only remaining `WiFi.softAP(...)` grep hit is inside a disabled `#if 0` test helper.
